metadata.name: FERS Retirement Eligibility Rules
metadata.description: FERS Minimum Retirement Age (MRA) schedule by birth year, complete eligibility matrix for all FERS retirement types, and VERA/VSIP early retirement provisions. Supplements fers-computation-rules.json (which covers annuity formulas) with the eligibility prerequisites.

mra_schedule._comment: Minimum Retirement Age by birth year. MRA is the earliest age at which a FERS employee can retire with an immediate annuity (with sufficient service). Does NOT apply to CSRS.

immediate_retirement._comment: Eligibility for immediate annuity — starts within 30 days of separation.

standard_pathways.description: Minimum Retirement Age with 30+ years of service
standard_pathways._note: Most favorable standard pathway — full unreduced annuity.
No SRS — employee is too close to age 62 SS eligibility.
Qualifies for 1.1% multiplier (enhanced formula) if 20+ years.

standard_pathways.description: Minimum Retirement Age with 10-29 years of service
standard_pathways.reduction_basis: 5% per year under age 62 (5/12 of 1% per month)
standard_pathways.reduction_elimination: Reduction eliminated at age 62, or at age 60 if 20+ years of service
standard_pathways.postponement_note: Employee may separate and postpone annuity commencement to reduce or eliminate penalty. FEHB and FEGLI cannot be continued during postponement period.
standard_pathways._note: Reduced annuity. Employee can also choose to postpone commencement.

special_category_retirement._comment: Enhanced retirement provisions for law enforcement officers, firefighters, and air traffic controllers under 5 U.S.C. § 8412(d)-(e).

standard.description: Age 50 with 20 years of special category service

any_age.description: Any age with 25 years of special category service

mandatory_retirement.exception: Agency head may approve extension to age 60 in public interest

special_category_retirement._note: Special category employees use the enhanced 1.7%/1.0% formula (see fers-computation-rules.json). The 1.1% age-62 enhancement does NOT apply.

vera.description: Agency-requested OPM authority to offer early retirement during major reorganization, reduction in force, or transfer of function.
vera.srs_note: SRS payable once employee reaches MRA. If already at or past MRA, SRS begins immediately.
vera.fehb_rule: Must have been enrolled in FEHB for 5 years immediately preceding retirement (or since first opportunity). OPM may grant pre-approved waivers.
vera.sick_leave_credit: 100% of unused sick leave credited for annuity computation (separations after 12/31/2013)
vera.reemployment_note: No restriction on non-federal employment. Federal reemployment as annuitant triggers salary offset rules.
vera._note: VERA must be approved by OPM for the specific agency and situation. Not a standing authority.

vsip.description: Lump-sum payment (up to $25,000) offered to employees who voluntarily separate during agency restructuring. Can be combined with VERA.
vsip.repayment_note: Employee must repay VSIP if rehired by the federal government within 5 years of separation.
vsip.repayment_waiver: Head of agency may waive repayment if individual possesses unique skills needed by the agency.
vsip.fehb_rule: Same 5-year enrollment rule as VERA. OPM waivers available.
vsip._note: VSIP does not require VERA — can be offered to employees who are not retirement-eligible. Non-retirement-eligible employees who accept VSIP simply separate.

involuntary_early.description: Early retirement for employees involuntarily separated (not for cause) during RIF, reorganization, or transfer of function.
involuntary_early._note: Same eligibility as VERA but triggered by involuntary separation rather than voluntary offer.

deferred_retirement.description: Annuity payable at a future date for employees who separate before meeting immediate retirement eligibility.

commencement_options.description: Deferred annuity commences at age 62 with 5+ years of service
Deferred annuity commences at MRA with 10+ years of service
commencement_options.reduction_note: Same 5% per year under 62 reduction as MRA+10 immediate retirement

deferred_retirement.fehb_note: FEHB coverage does NOT continue during the deferral period. Coverage can resume when deferred annuity commences if the 5-year enrollment requirement was met before separation.
deferred_retirement.sick_leave_note: Unused sick leave is NOT credited for deferred retirements. Lost at separation.
deferred_retirement.srs_note: SRS is NOT payable with deferred retirement.

disability_retirement._comment: Eligibility only — computation rules are in fers-computation-rules.json.
disability_retirement.service_note: 18 months of creditable civilian service under FERS
requirements: Must be disabled for useful and efficient service in current position
Disability expected to last at least one year
Agency must certify it cannot accommodate the disability in the current position or any vacant position at the same grade/pay within the same agency and commuting area
Must not have declined a reasonable offer of reassignment
disability_retirement.filing_deadline: Application must be filed before separation or within one year after separation
disability_retirement._note: See fers-computation-rules.json for the 60%/40% disability formula and age-62 recomputation.

fehb_five_year_rule._comment: Critical eligibility requirement for continuing FEHB into retirement.
fehb_five_year_rule.description: To continue FEHB coverage in retirement, an employee must have been continuously enrolled in any FEHB plan for the 5 years of service immediately preceding retirement, or since the employee's first opportunity to enroll (if less than 5 years).
exceptions: OPM may waive the 5-year requirement for VERA/VSIP separations
Waiver automatically granted if employee was continuously enrolled since the start of the agency's approved VERA/VSIP authority
fehb_five_year_rule._note: Losing FEHB in retirement is one of the most financially significant oversights in federal retirement planning.
